Point of View

How I Think About Product

The best product decisions I've made weren't about features. They were about the system underneath the features — the architecture that makes the next feature cheaper, the org structure that makes the next team faster, the framework that turns one product into a platform.

I use hypothesis-driven development not as a methodology checkbox but as a forcing function: if we can't articulate what we're trying to learn, we shouldn't be building it yet. I invest in discovery before roadmaps, in extensibility before scale, and in team practices before team size.

Most of all, I believe product leadership is organizational design. The way a product team is structured, the practices it runs, the language it uses — those things shape product quality more than any individual prioritization decision. That's where I spend my leverage.

About

I'm a product executive who builds infrastructure — not just products.

The systems I care about most aren't features. They're the frameworks that make future features faster, the org structures that make teams self-directing, and the platforms that compound in value long after the first launch.

Over 10+ years spanning fintech, tax technology, and enterprise SaaS, I've built zero-to-one products at Bloomberg Tax and MainStreet Bank, designed extensible architectures that cut delivery timelines by 3×, and founded organizational practices that elevated product maturity across entire business units.

I'm most useful when the problem is ambiguous, the stakes are high, and someone needs to see the system underneath the symptoms.
